Check for Stains or Problem Areas Before you start painting, carefully inspect your ceiling for water stains, mould spots, or cracks. Dust Sheets & Floor Protectors Protecting your floors and furniture from paint splatters is essential. Masking Tape High-quality painters tape is best for sharp, clean lines.

Use them along ceiling edges and cornices to prevent paint from seeping under the tape. Paintbrushes & Trays For edges and corners, an angled brush is ideal for cutting in around your ceiling’s edges.

Plan Your Approach Finally, plan your approach before you dive in. Decide the direction you’ll roll set clear start and finish points. Tips On How To Painting Your Ceiling Ceiling painting doesn’t just involve dipping your roller and getting started. There are a few techniques to follow if you want a smooth, streak-free finish: Work in Sections Instead of trying to paint the entire ceiling at once, break it down into manageable sections.

Instead, move the roller in smooth, parallel strokes, following a consistent direction rather than a back-and-forth motion. A slight W pattern works well too. Avoid Stopping in the Middle Start and stop at natural boundaries, like cornices, light fixtures, or structural edges. Stopping in the middle of an open area can leave visible lines that won’t blend well when the paint dries. Don’t Overwork the Paint Avoid overlapping already painted areas.

This helps you maintain control and ensures even coverage, preventing the paint from drying before you can blend the edges properly. Maintain a Wet Edge A wet edge helps avoid streaks and overlap marks. Try to apply fresh paint close to the area youve just finished, then roll back into the wet section to blend the two. Roll Slowly & in One Direction Rolling too quickly can create splatters and uneven texture.

Paint a border along the perimeter and around light fixtures. Working in small sections ensures the wet edges blend seamlessly when you begin rolling. DIY Or Professional Ceiling Painting.
